responseMessageContents.text:
The user is frustrated because the preview system is stuck showing an old version of their app. They want to know if they should open a new project and what to tell me to continue from where they left off.

Let me read the current state of the project to understand what exists.

I understand the frustration with the preview being stuck. Here's what you can do:

**Option 1: Open a new project on Rork**

When you create a new project, come back and tell me something like:

> "I have an existing app that needs to be migrated to this new project. Here's what it should have: [describe your app features]"

Or better yet, let me first check what your current app looks like so I can give you an exact description to paste:
Let me get a full picture of the app by reading key files so I can give the user a proper description to use in a new project.
Let me read your key files so I can give you a complete description to paste into a new project.

The user wants to know what to do if they open a new project. Let me read the key files to understand the full app, so I can give them a complete description to paste into the new project. Let me read the most important files.
Let me read your key files to understand your full app, so I can give you a complete prompt to use in a new project.

Now I have a full picture of your app. Here's what you should do:

---

**Create a new project on Rork**, then paste this message as your first prompt:

---

> **Build me an equipment rental marketplace app called "HeavyAr" (هيفي آر) for renting heavy equipment in Saudi Arabia.** The app uses Firebase for auth and Firestore for data, Cloudinary for image uploads, and supports Arabic/English bilingual with full RTL support.
> 
> **Tech stack:**
> - Firebase Auth (email/password login & registration)
> - Firestore for data (equipment, requests, chat, ratings, invoices)
> - Cloudinary for image uploads
> - Arabic (default) + English language toggle with RTL support
> - `@nkzw/create-context-hook` for AuthContext and LanguageContext
> - AsyncStorage for caching user profile and language preference
> - React Query for data fetching
> 
> **Tabs (5 tabs):**
> 1. **Home** - Welcome greeting (personalized if logged in), search bar, categories horizontal scroll, featured equipment, recent equipment. Header shows language selector (globe icon) for guests, bell icon for authenticated users, plus app logo.
> 2. **Search** - Search/filter equipment by category, region, city, price range
> 3. **Add** (center gold plus button) - Create new equipment listing (requires auth, provider role)
> 4. **Requests** - View rental requests (sent/received based on role)
> 5. **Profile** - User profile, settings, my equipment, invoices, logout
> 
> **Additional screens (outside tabs as stack):**
> - Equipment detail page `/equipment/[id]` - full details, image gallery, request rental button
> - Request detail `/request/[id]` - request info, accept/reject for providers
> - Chat `/chat/[requestId]` - messaging between customer and provider
> - Payment `/payment/[requestId]` (modal) - payment flow
> - Rating `/rating/[requestId]` (modal) - rate after completion
> - Login & Register (modals) - with role selection (customer/provider), CR number for providers, region/city selection from Saudi regions list
> - Onboarding screen
> - Settings screen
> - My Equipment management screen
> - Edit Equipment screen
> - Create Listing screen (modal)
> - Invoices screen
> 
> **Data types:**
> - User: uid, nameAr, nameEn, email, phone, avatar, region, city, customCity, role (customer/provider), crNumber, crVerified, rating, totalRatings, equipmentCount, joinedAt, isVerified
> - Equipment: id, ownerUid, titleAr, titleEn, descriptionAr, descriptionEn, category, region, city, customCity, district, location (lat/lng), pricePerDay, images, availability, isActive
> - EquipmentRequest: id, equipmentId, customerUid, providerUid, status (pending/accepted/in_progress/completed/rejected/cancelled), requestMode (fixed_days/open_ended), numberOfDays, startDate, endDate, notes, amount, platformFee, providerAmount, paymentStatus, allowChat
> - ChatMessage, Rating, Invoice types
> - Categories with Arabic and English names
> 
> **Design:** Dark theme with gold (#D4AF37) accent color. Professional, clean mobile-native design. Saudi regions data for location selection.
> 
> **Environment variables I have:** EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY, EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN, EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID, EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET, EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID, EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_APP_ID, EXPO_PUBLIC_CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME, CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN

---

Copy-paste that entire block into the new project's first message. It describes your full app so I can rebuild it from scratch in the new project with a clean preview system.
